statement: The use of δικαιόω in James 2:24 is compatible with its use in Romans 3:28 through proper understanding of justification context

profileSensitivity.notes: Relies heavily on text-critical assumptions about James canonicity (Catholic/Orthodox vs Protestant) and semantic range of τελειόω

passages.text: Διὸ λογιζόμεθα πίστει δικαιοῦσθαι ἄνθρωπον χωρὶς ἔργων νόμου
ὁρατε ὅτι ἐξ ἔργων δικαιοῦται ἄνθρωπος καὶ οὐκ ἐκ πίστεως μόνον
Ye see then how that by works a man is justified, and not by faith only

researchNotes: Orthodox view requires consulting primary texts in Greek patristic collections about theosis and synergism (Athanasius, Chrysostom)
